channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Frontend-Only SaaS: The Rise of Static Utility Sites
item.description: Frontend-Only SaaS The combination of fast browser APIs (Web Crypto, Workers, WebAssembly) and generous third-party APIs (mail.gw, webhook.site) means you can ship serious developer tooling with...

item.title: What I Learned About Token Design on Solana as a Web2 Developer
item.description: A week ago my Solana tokens were just random strings of characters. No name, no rules, no personality. By the end of this week I had built tokens that charge their own fees, tokens that refuse to...

item.title: What I learned putting my own text editor into production
item.description: A follow-up to "The 5.2 kB editor I had to write because nothing else fit." Shipping a package to npm is one thing. Actually using it in the production app it was built for is a different exercise...

item.title: How to Build Efficient Context for Your AI Client to handle incidents
item.description: Most teams that adopt an AI coding or operations assistant eventually reach the same conclusion: The model is rarely the bottleneck. The bottleneck is context. A capable model with the wrong...

item.title: Why I Started Using HTMX Alongside Vue.js (And Didn't Expect to Like It)
item.description: A few months ago, if someone had asked me whether I'd use HTMX, my answer would've been pretty simple: "Probably not. I already use Vue." I wasn't looking for another frontend tool. Vue already...

item.title: Part 1-3: From Raw Health Text to a Working Retrieval Pipeline
item.description: I'm a first-year undergraduate student in Artificial Intelligence Engineering, and this summer I'm building a small RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) project from scratch to learn how these...

item.title: Backtracking: Solving Sudoku Like Neo in The Matrix
item.description: The Quest Begins (The "Why") I still remember the first time I stared at a blank Sudoku grid during a mock interview. The interviewer slid the paper over, smiled, and said, “Just fill it in.” My...

item.title: Two false walls in NOAA's currents API: empty constituents and "not available"
item.description: This one started with a snap judgment, and the snap judgment was wrong in the most useful way. I was looking at XTide, the reference-grade open-source tide and current predictor, and my first...

item.title: Kubernetes HPA: Guide with Apache on KIND
item.description: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) automatically increases or decreases the number of Pods based on CPU or memory utilization. In this project, I configured HPA on a local KIND cluster and verified...

item.title: Stop Hiring a Rocket Scientist to Check If a Box Is Empty
item.description: One thing I've been thinking about lately is what companies are willing to sacrifice just to have in their title "AI-powered". A tale as old as 2023 A team needs to validate an email address....

item.title: Corsair’s PC case with a panoramic glass design is $70 off
item.description: On the hunt for a PC case that’s loaded with features and has a unique look? Amazon, Best Buy, and Corsair are selling the Corsair Frame 4500X RS for $119.99, a $70 discount from the usual price....

item.title: Tesla’s robotaxi promises are clashing with reality
item.description: In an earnings call yesterday, Tesla CEO Elon Musk did his best to paint a positive portrait of the company's robotaxi program. New cities are being added, more miles are being driven, and more...

item.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
item.description: Runway no longer wants to be just another AI model company. It wants to become the infrastructure layer for generative media. On Thursday, the startup launched Runway Media Router through Runway...

item.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all U.S. users
item.description: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

item.title: Geekbench 7 will push your computer or phone even harder for better benchmarking
item.description: Primate Labs is releasing Geekbench 7, the latest generation of its popular benchmarking tool. Geekbench 7 features new video and audio encoding / decoding tests, a redesigned multi-core test, and...

item.title: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out ChatGPT Health to everyone in the US on Thursday, allowing more people to connect their medical records and health-tracking information to the chatbot. During a briefing,...

item.title: Supercooled kidneys have been transplanted into pigs in a “landmark achievement”
item.description: When it comes to organ donation, time is everything. As soon as an organ has been carefully removed from a donor’s body, it starts to deteriorate. Surgeons have a matter of hours to get it into a...

item.title: Amazon puts Luna cloud-streamed games like Fallout 4 inside Prime Video
item.description: The new strategy that Amazon gaming exec Jeff Gattis talked to The Verge about last month is getting clearer now that Prime Video has added a new Games tab on Fire TV devices. With Prime Gaming,...

item.title: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
item.description: David Bowie's song "Five Years," which Meta used in a supposedly inspiring advertisement, is about humans learning that they have five years left to live before the apocalypse.

item.title: Microsoft is testing free, ad-supported cloud gaming for Xbox Insiders
item.description: Xbox Insiders can stream games from their libraries for free in ad-supported streaming sessions starting today. The free streaming sessions are limited to games you already own, and sessions are...

item.title: ThreatsDay: Android Spyware, PLC Attacks, AI Image Prompt Injection + 12 More Stories
item.description: Most of this week's trouble came dressed as something useful. A package stole data. A fake extension opened remote access. A safety app became spyware. An image gave hidden orders to an AI agent....

item.title: Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
item.description: If there's a place in the universe without GPUs, Nvidia is sending them there.

item.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
item.description: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

item.title: The new Halo remake is a reminder of what Xbox used to be
item.description: It's impossible to talk about a new Xbox game without also talking about the state of Xbox. Microsoft's gaming division is in freefall: Recent headlines are dominated by extensive layoffs,...

item.title: Amazon’s best 4K streaming sticks are up to 40 percent off
item.description: There’s no shortage of great shows and movies to watch, but discovering something new isn’t always easy. Amazon’s Fire TV Stick 4K Plus and Fire TV Stick 4K Max come with redesigned software that...

item.title: Google’s Gemini nears billion-user milestone
item.description: Gemini had over 750 million monthly users in February.

item.title: Lawmakers prepare bill requiring AI ‘kill switch’
item.description: Lawmakers are preparing to introduce an "AI Kill Switch Act" that would require AI companies to shut down or throttle their systems on orders from the Department of Homeland Security, according to...

item.title: Apple’s OpenAI lawsuit is about who gets to define the post-smartphone era
item.description: Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Hayden Field, The Verge’s senior AI reporter, about the major trade secrets lawsuit between Apple and OpenAI and what this tells us about OpenAI’s future. By now...

item.title: Claude Cowork Flaw Could Let AI Agent Escape Its VM and Access Mac Files
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a sandbox escape vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Cowork that makes it possible to break out of the confines of a Linux virtual machine (VM) within...

item.title: Chaos Ransomware Uses msaRAT to Route C2 Traffic Through Headless Chrome and Edge
item.description: The Chaos ransomware group ran its command-and-control through the victim's own browser. Cisco Talos on Thursday detailed msaRAT, the Rust implant behind it, found on a compromised Windows machine...
